response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong breadth (web, mobile, embedded, testing culture) and some relevant systems exposure through low-latency DLang microservices at Dunnhumby, but he clearly misses the core requirement of 8 years of C++ — his C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js product work rather than large-scale cloud compute infrastructure, and there is no evidence of sustained technical-lead responsibility for platform strategy, mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers, or owning large infrastructure programs at the level this role demands.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput microservices experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-craft background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching/workshop experience showing mentoring aptitude
Broad polyglot experience and open-source ownership (GISCollective on Kubernetes) demonstrating ability to drive projects end-to-end
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement (~7 months professional C/C++)
Career focus is web/product full-stack, not cloud compute platforms, storage, or hardware-adjacent infrastructure
No formal technical-leadership track record leading cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables
